reviews.review_msg: Wouldn't recommend this teacher to any student taking an Asian Studies class at SFU since she gives nonconstructive feedback w/ regards to how arguments should be supported, ideas should be structured, or meanings should be interpreted.  Consistently grades written assignments on a personal emotional whim if your ideas are not aligned with hers.

The class context is okay; however, the professor will make you do a group presentation, and she does not care about your work. She will give the grade based on the overall performance of the group. Similar to the Discussion, which is heavily graded is in a group. It is hard to ask for her help. She is barely available, even by email.
A great class to take if you interest in the Asian novel and culture.

In my view, Professor Kong made the course very interesting and engaging. The novels used in this course may be difficult for some students to read and grasps the main concepts, but it was very easy to follow as she provides weekly reading responses/discussions. Overall, I would love to take another class with Professor Kong. She's a great prof!

Professor Kong is intelligent, and passionate about the content she uses in lecture. I took a 6-week intensive course with her and on day one I thought I would die. I didn't. I studied hard and came prepared, raised concerns when they arrived, and she helped me with everything. Her accent is not an issue, that's just a rude comment from students.

Knowlegable professor, has a clear view of Asian (Chinese) literature and society. However, need improve on how to educate ppl. Unclear grading spec, you never know why she cut off your mark. Participation is 50%, try to be active in class. Recommend study group for your own discussion because she won't give you enough time to do it in class.
inspiring content. She engages the students in issues relevant to Asian Canadian. Totally enjoy the class atmosphere.

Strongly not recommend. Extremely unfair grading policy in this course. The prof grades only on her personal feeling on every student and once your idea did not meet hers. You will get an unbelievable low mark. She usually interrupts you several times during your presentation and she did not care presentation skill. Express your own idea means low

"Contemporary Chinese Literature" was all about learning to read in historical context and with human sensitivity. Prof. Kong clearly cares about her students and is all about making us better readers. This class would be an excellent choice for westerners wanting to learn to appreciate a large minority culture in Greater Vancouver.
